cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So the question is about figuring out when Tiffany could have gone to the market, based on the times different people saw her doing various things. The market is closed after 10pm, so we need to find a time when she wasn't doing any of the other activities mentioned. First, let me list out all the known activities with their times. - Tiffany woke up at 6am. Well, that just tells us she was awake then, but maybe not necessarily doing anything else yet. But Sarah saw her reading at the library from 6am to 9am. So that's one activity: 6am to 9am at the library. Then Betty saw her driving to the water park from 9am to 10am. So that's 9am-10am. Then Linda saw her buying a phone at the electronics store from 12pm to 6pm. Wait, that's a long time—12pm to 6pm. So from noon until 6 in the evening. Then Mary saw her working out at the gym from 6pm to 10pm. So that's 6pm to 10pm. The market was closed after 10pm, so she must have gone before that. Now, we need to see the times when Tiffany was not doing any of these activities. Let's map out the timeline: - 6am to 9am: at library - 9am to 10am: driving to water park - Then from 10am to 12pm: what's she doing? Not mentioned. After 10am, the next activity is at 12pm. So between 10am and 12pm, she's unaccounted for. Then from 12pm to 6pm: electronics store. Then from 6pm to 10pm: gym. So the only time slots where she's not doing anything are between 10am-12pm and maybe after 10pm. But the market is closed after 10pm, so she can't go then. So the only available time is between 10am to 12pm. Looking at the options, option B is 10am to 12pm. Wait, let me check the options again: A) 6am to 9am – she was at the library, so no. B) 10am to 12pm – that's the gap between driving
